plan.reasoning:
SMILES Consistency Check: The SMILES [N]=N represents diazene (N2H0 radical form) or more precisely a nitrogen radical species. From the RDKit analysis: atom 0 is N with SP2 hybridization, degree 1, and 1 radical electron; atom 1 is N with SP2 hybridization, degree 1, and 0 radical electrons. This is a reactive radical species (N2 diradical/NxHy fragment). Since atom 0 has radical_electrons=1, it is the reactive binding atom.

Adsorbate Analysis: [N]=N is a dinitrogen species with a radical on atom 0 (N). This is best treated as a ReactiveSpecies binding through the radical nitrogen (index 0).

Surface Analysis: The surface contains Sr and Ag atoms. Available sites include ontop (Ag, Sr), bridge (Ag-Ag, Ag-Sr, Sr-Sr), and hollow (Ag-Ag-Sr). For N2-like species, end-on adsorption at an ontop site is the most common and energetically favorable configuration on metal surfaces. Sr is more electropositive and may bind N more strongly.

Plan: Since this is a first attempt (no history), I will start with the most physically motivated configuration: end-on adsorption of the radical N (index 0) at an ontop Sr site, as Sr is more reactive toward N species than Ag. This is a ReactiveSpecies with the radical on atom 0 binding to Sr ontop.
